description_long: This is a large, flexible yellow banner with bold, hand-painted black text reading 'POWER TO SOLAR' across its surface. The banner is made of a fabric-like material, giving it a soft, slightly wrinkled appearance with natural folds and curves. The text is unevenly spaced and painted in a rough, expressive style, adding a sense of urgency and activism. The edges of the banner are irregular and not perfectly straight, suggesting it is not tightly stretched or mounted. There are no visible mounting points, grommets, or support structures, making it appear as if the banner is freely suspended or caught in motion. The overall look is that of a protest or advocacy banner, likely intended for outdoor urban environments.
description: A large, yellow fabric banner with bold black hand-painted text reading 'POWER TO SOLAR', featuring natural folds and a soft, flexible appearance.
description_view_0: The front of the banner shows the full 'POWER TO SOLAR' text clearly, with visible folds and a wavy, dynamic shape.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the thinness of the banner and its pronounced curvature, with only partial text visible along the edge.
description_view_2: The back of the banner displays the text in reverse, with the same yellow color and fabric texture, and the folds are more pronounced.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, showing the banner's thin profile and curvature, with some text visible along the edge.
